lines: // Env is environment variables added to shim processes
+	// SocketDir is the directory to place shim sockets. The path must be
+	// short enough to fit within the platform's unix socket path limit.
+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("socket_dir must be an absolute path: %q", config.SocketDir)
+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("socket_dir length must be no longer than %d characters", maxSocketDirLen)
+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to find a suitable socket directory for shim, please configure one")
@@ -45,3 +48,78 @@ func checkCopyShimLogError(ctx context.Context, err error) error {
+// defaultSocketDir returns the directory used for shim unix sockets.
+// The path is intentionally kept short and hardcoded rather than derived
+// from the configured state directory, because unix socket paths are
+// limited to 104-108 characters. If no suitable path can be found,
+//  1. The default state directory (/run/containerd/s) — try to create it,
+//     and if it already exists verify the current user owns it.
+//     and owned by the current user.
+//     exists and is owned by the current user.
+//  4. /tmp/containerd-s-<UID> — created and ownership-verified as a last resort.
+	// All candidates failed, return empty string and let caller handle
+// ensureSocketDir attempts to create dir with mode 0700, verifies it is
+// owned by uid, and corrects the permissions to 0700 if they differ.
+// Returns true if the directory is ready for use.
@@ -93,3 +95,7 @@ func checkCopyShimLogError(ctx context.Context, err error) error {

+// maxSocketDirLen is the maximum length of the socket directory path.
+// Unix socket paths are limited to 108 characters on Linux, minus a
+// null terminator gives 107 usable characters. The socket path passed
+// to the kernel is directory + "/" (1) + sha256 hash (64); the
+// "unix://" scheme is stripped before net.Listen and does not count.
+// So the directory can be at most 107 - 1 - 64 = 42.

+// maxSocketDirLen is the maximum length of the socket directory path.

+// "unix://" scheme is stripped before net.Listen and does not count.
+// So the directory can be at most 103 - 1 - 64 = 38.

+// scheme is stripped before net.Listen and does not count.
